# Deferred Quality Levers for the POI-MTL Champion

This catalogues the candidate **quality** improvements surfaced — and deliberately set aside — during the `train_perf_multifold` refactor, which was a quality-**neutral** (byte-identical) effort. None of these belong in that refactor; every one changes model outputs, so **parity (`parity_check.sh`) is necessary but NOT sufficient** — each needs an n=20 multi-seed `{0,1,7,100}×5f` A/B vs the champion to gate. Champion-G baseline = `RESULTS_BOARD §1` (dual-tower `next_stan_flow_dualtower` reg + `next_gru` cat on `check2hgi_dk_ovl`, static_weight cw=0.75, prior-OFF / KD-OFF, true fp32 on A40).

## Ranked table

| # | Lever | Verdict | Expected effect (metric ± mag, confidence) | Breaking risk | Effort |
|---|-------|---------|--------------------------------------------|---------------|--------|
| 1 | **fp32 (corrected-precision) MTL default board-wide** | real_quality_lever | reg Acc@10 **+0.2 pp AL** (gap −0.38→−0.18) … **~+1.7 pp / gap-reversal FL** (fold-1 77.71>76.71); cat ~neutral; dir high-conf, mag moderate | High — re-baseline all MTL cells (STL ceilings already fp32, stand) | medium |

---

## (A) Real quality levers worth piloting

### A1 — Make true-fp32 the MTL default board-wide *(real_quality_lever — ADOPT)*
- **Change:** Extend `_auto_fp32_for_large_c` (currently C>2000, `mtl_cv.py:1044-1057`) to small states too, or set `MTL_DISABLE_AMP=1` for all MTL board cells. No new code (env var exists).
- **Why:** MTL trains under **fp16 autocast with NO GradScaler** (`mtl_cv.py:1178-1208`; only NashMTL backward is fp32). The STL reg ceiling (`p1_region_head_ablation.py:83`) was **always true-fp32** → the original "MTL sacrifices reg" Δreg was an **fp16-MTL-vs-fp32-ceiling mismatch**, a correctness artifact, not a real gap.
- **Evidence:** Measured on champion-G (dk_ovl, seed0, 5f gated overlap): `CA_MTL_DIVERGENCE.md:128-166` (AL 69.60→69.80, FL fold-1 77.71 > ceiling 76.71, cat unchanged 63.44→63.48); `BOARD_H100_FINDINGS.md:86-94` (AL/AZ/FL fp32 cells beat both FL ceilings); `RESULTS_BOARD.md:104-107` (bf16≈fp32). Cat is precision-insensitive.
- **Gate:** Full-5f **multi-seed {0,1,7,100} A/B** at FL + CA (the reversal is currently single-fold seed-0) before claiming gap-reversal; AL retains a real −0.18 residual (closes, not erases). Re-baseline scope = **MTL cells only** (STL ceilings already fp32 and stand).
- **Effort:** medium (re-gate MTL cells × states × seeds; A40-feasible per-state).

---

## (B) Contested / champion-regime-dependent

These genuinely helped a *different* regime (single-pathway / small states) and are null-or-harmful on the dual-tower champion — pilot scoped, never a global default.

### B1 — log_T-KD W=0.2 (τ=1.0) **at AL/AZ only**, on dk_ovl *(contested)*
- **Change:** `--log-t-kd-weight 0.2` for AL/AZ reg head; keep **0.0 at FL/CA/TX**.
- **Why / split:** Paper-grade small-state win — AL **+2.27** / AZ **+4.91** reg (n=20, p=9.5e-7, 20/20 folds, leak-audited; `F_TIER_A1_PROMOTION.md`, `CH26`). But at FL the dual-tower reg is already well-fit: **+0.04 reg / −0.70 cat** Pareto-bad (`mtl_frontier/FINDINGS.md:618-651`). The +1.4…+2.4 large-state pilots were the OLD single-pathway B9 regime, NOT dual-tower G.
- **Gate:** Pilot **AL/AZ n=20 on dk_ovl** (the validated lift is on v11/v14, not dk_ovl). MUST build leak-free seeded `region_transition_log_seed{S}_fold{N}.pt` per seed against dk_ovl (board currently points `--per-fold-transition-dir` at the inert design_k dir). Wrong seed/splits/engine inflates reg +8…+12 pp (`feedback_p1_default_logT_leaks`).
- **Effort:** medium.

### B2 — Alternating per-batch optimizer step on G *(contested)*
- **Change:** `--alternating-optimizer-step` (even=cat-only, odd=reg-only; `mtl_cv.py:1476-1488`). Run **accum=1** for a clean test (accum-2 mixes cat+reg batches in one group).
- **Why:** +4.04 pp reg paper-grade (Wilcoxon p=0.0312) — but **only on single-pathway `mtlnet_crossattn`** (`NORTH_STAR.md:253`, `F51`). On G the mechanism is undercut: tasks are gradient-orthogonal (cos∇≈+0.0008 over 16 runs, `FINAL_SYNTHESIS.md:63-67`) and the reg STAN tower already sits at its STL ceiling, so cat-dominance collapse is solved architecturally.
- **Gate:** FL + AL/AZ **n=20 Pareto A/B** (static_weight cw=0.75, alt-opt on/off). Expect null on dual-tower.
- **Effort:** medium.

### B3 — Faithful-STAN geometric matching layer in the MTL reg head *(contested)*
- **Change:** Bring STAN's faithful geometric matching layer into the reg tower. **Do NOT** touch sequence construction (prefix-expansion decouples reg seqs from shared windowing → breaks the W6 +4.6…+7.6 pp cat lift, `W6_ENCODER_ISOLATION.md`).
- **Why:** Champion reg head already wraps full-STAN (`next_stan_flow_dualtower`). The `STAN-stl_hgi` headroom is largely a **precision artifact** — fp32 MTL FL 77.71 > STAN-stl_hgi 76.82 (reverses) — and a **substrate confound** (HGI wins STL next-reg; champion runs dk_ovl). Geometric matching needs raw coords absent from `next_region.parquet`.
- **Gate:** First complete A1 (fp32 re-baseline). Only if a residual fp32 gap persists, pilot the **matching layer alone** at AL/AZ n=20.
- **Effort:** high.

---

## (C) Robustness / correctness hardening (bit-identical now)

Land as defensive hardening, NOT paper experiments — they cannot move cat/reg/joint on current data. The only valid "A/B" is an AL champion-G byte-identical Acc@10 **assert** (a nonzero delta would itself flag a hidden bug).

### C1 — STAN U1: explicit last-True pooling *(speculative — bit-identical)*
- **Change:** Replace count-based `last_idx = num_valid-1` with `S-1 - valid[:,::-1].argmax` in `next_stan/head.py:165-168` (+ residual site `:325-326`). The layout-agnostic form already exists at `next_region.py:137-141`.
- **Why:** Both forms are equal only for **tail-padded** sequences; data IS left-packed (`core.py:262-263` pads at end). U1 hardens against any future interior/right-pad builder. `AUDIT_FINDINGS.md:36-45` files it under "#4 NO BUG / recommended hardening, bit-identical now."
- **Gate:** AL champion-G byte-identical Acc@10 no-op assert. **Effort:** low.

### C2 — STAN U2: padded-row softmax-index alignment *(speculative — bit-identical)*
- **Change:** Fix the fully-padded-row case (`last_idx` reads index 0 vs masked-softmax all-`-inf`→NaN, `head.py:150-168`). Fold in with U1 for free.
- **Why:** Unreachable today (`MIN_SEQUENCE_LENGTH` + sliding-window guarantee ≥1 valid step). Pure robustness. **Effort:** low.

### C3 — fp32-attention island `MTL_STAN_FP32_ATTN=1` *(speculative — keep merged + OFF)*
- **Change:** Already implemented (`head.py:63-70,130-144`), gated on `is_autocast_enabled` → **no-op under the fp32 board protocol**. Keep merged, default OFF.
- **Why:** Targets the A40-Ampere bf16 backward grad-NaN (CA/TX, C=6.5–8.5k). Root cause is a **hypothesis** (suspected STAN softmax row), **unvalidated on the real NaN case** — AL (C=1109) can't reproduce it, H100 can't either. The void already has a **confirmed fix** (true fp32, `MTL_DISABLE_AMP=1` → TX reg 67.02), so no quality gap remains on the board.
- **Gate:** Only if A40 bf16 throughput ever becomes the bottleneck: one A40 large-state **TX bf16 run** asserting 0 non-finite skips + reg≈67.02 (vs void −2.37). **Effort:** low.

### C4 — Composite-key stratification (stay-rate × modal category) (#2) *(speculative — future-works only)*
- **Change:** Stratify on a composite key before `StratifiedGroupKFold` (`folds.py:1371-1383`).
- **Why:** Strong measured correlations on the frozen seed-0 split (stay-rate↔reg@10 r=+0.907, entropy↔cat-F1 r=+0.973); fold-5 deficit is "structural CV variance, not a bug." It would shrink fold variance but **shifts no head mean** (variance redistribution only) and its payoff is *projected, not measured*.
- **Breaking risk:** **HIGH** — redraws every seed's user→fold split → all §0.1/board numbers shift → full re-gate + log_T regen. The n=20 grid already tightens the CI without touching the split.
- **Gate:** ONLY as a **labeled future-works robustness ablation** on a NEW versioned split — never a silent swap. **Effort:** medium.

---

## (D) Speculative / skip (one line each)

**Refuted (measured null/negative on champion-G — do not re-pilot):**
- **α·log_T prior activation** — reg DOWN 0.5–3 pp (grows with C); private tower subsumes it; on the do-not-retry list (`CHAMPION.md §5`).
- **R1 log_C co-location KD** — AL reg +0.207±0.196 FAILS ≥0.3 gate, FL null, cat −0.27; redundant with log_T Markov prior (`mtl_frontier R1`, CLOSED null).
- **R3 reverse cat-KD** — cat +0.10±0.28 AL (p=0.31, one seed neg), FL null; output-prior family saturated (`r3_rev_al_multiseed_results.json`).
- **log_T-KD stacked / promoted on G** — FL 4-seed reg +0.04 / cat −0.70; the small-state lift was a v11 single-pathway artifact (X2 re-test after aux-gate fix, NULL).
- **Calibrated cat loss (logit-adjust/focal/CB)** — cat −0.87/−2.71/−1.72; plain CE is the proven MTL cat optimum (`mtl_improvement T2V.7`); STL win doesn't transfer (rising-tide).
- **Loss-scale normalization** — FL reg −37.8 pp degenerate head, cw re-tune never recovers; EXCLUDED in two independent verdicts.
- **reg_freeze_at_epoch** — cat null (≤+0.46), reg HURT (AL N=2 −7.69); Tier-C2 ARCHIVE.
- **Scheduler swap (warmup_constant / reg_head_warmup_decay)** — reg −1.8…−5.2 pp; per-head LR (already used) wins.
- **design_k(gcn) substrate / dual-substrate routing** — MTL reg washes out (v14≈canonical), routing falsified (47.17≈47.15), +cat penalty.
- **Directed cat→region cascade (CSLSL)** — Δjoint ≤0.02 ≪ fold-std (dead tie, `RESULTS_BOARD §1b`).
- **Delaunay live reg prior** — ~0 FL (reg at ceiling), AL sub-gate; STL Delaunay washes out in MTL (regime finding).
- **POI2Vec live teacher → reg tower** — reg washout + cat downside; live-input = gradient interference (`Design E`).

**Perf-not-quality (mislabeled speed knobs — skip as quality):**
- **SDPA in `_STANAttention`** — ~0 at S=9 (9×9 attn), reduction-order re-gate cost; real bf16 fix is the fp32 island.
- **Fused/foreach AdamW** — ~0 expected, unknown sign; FP-reorder noise = fold-std.
- **bf16-default / remove AMP gate** — neutral when stable, NaN-void (reg 62.59) on A40 large-C; keep bf16 opt-in for H100 throughput only.
- **num_workers>0** — no quality gain, AL drift +0.92/+0.23 is noise; OOMs FL; breaks RNG byte-identity.

**Already default (shipped — no action):**
- **Non-finite-grad skip guard** — default-ON (`mtl_cv.py:16-37`, commit 27b4bc5a), no-op on healthy runs.
- **fp16 GradScaler** — superseded by committed bf16 + auto-fp32; keeps fp16 mantissa understatement, ~0 gain.
- **v14/ResLN substrate for MTL** — champion already trains on v14 (`§v16`); substrate axis washed out under cross-attn MTL. (Residual real work: CA/TX v14 **build** = enabling-infra to RUN G there, not a quality lever.)

---

## Recommended next experiments (highest expected value, A40-feasible)

1. **fp32 MTL re-baseline (A1)** — the one real quality lever and a correctness fix. Extend auto-fp32 to small states / `MTL_DISABLE_AMP=1` board-wide; full-5f **n=20 {0,1,7,100}** at FL + CA to confirm the gap close/reversal (currently single-fold seed-0). Cat-neutral, closes the central reg gap. *(medium)*
2. **log_T-KD AL/AZ-only pilot on dk_ovl (B1)** — re-confirm the validated +2.27/+4.91 reg lift survives on the dk_ovl substrate (was proven on v11/v14). Build leak-free seeded per-fold log_T first; AL/AZ are small → cheap. Keep OFF at FL/CA/TX. *(medium)*
3. **Land U1+U2+composite-key as hardening (C1/C2)** — one-line index swaps + AL byte-identical Acc@10 assert. Near-zero cost, removes latent interior/right-pad and all-pad-row footguns. *(low)*
4. **Alternating-optimizer A/B on dual-tower G (B2)** — FL+AL/AZ n=20, accum=1. Cheap falsification of the single-pathway +4.04 reg win on the new architecture (expect null). *(medium)*
5. **(Conditional on A1) Faithful-STAN matching-layer pilot (B3)** — only if a residual fp32 reg gap persists at AL/AZ; matching layer alone, never sequence construction (W6). *(high — defer)*

All of B/C beyond parity require an **n=20 multi-seed A/B vs champion-G** with a ≥0.3 pp promote gate — parity alone is insufficient because these change quality by construction.
